title: Bridge Essay: Neural mechanisms underlying visual pareidolia processing: An fMRI study.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal** (Phase 10) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Imaginal to mechanistic neuroscience through Neural, mechanisms, underlying, visual, pareidolia.

**Neural mechanisms underlying visual pareidolia processing: An fMRI study.** — Pak J Med Sci. PMID: 30559823. DOI: 10.12669/pjms.346.16140. Pareidolia is the interpretation of previously unseen and unrelated objects as familiar due to previous learning. The present study aimed to determine the specific brain areas that exhibited activation during real-face and face-pareidolia processing. Functional Magnetic Resonance Imaging (fMRI) scans were performed on 20 healthy subjects under real-face and face-pareidolia conditions in National Magnetic Resonance Research Center (UMRAM), Ankara, Turkey from April 2016 to January 2017.
